intro: Led product for Senza, Synamedia's cloud-based streaming TV platform. Owned the full developer experience — documentation, sample apps, SDK features, platform tooling, and analytics — that enabled customers to integrate, launch, and scale their streaming applications.
bullets: Owned the SDK, developer console, and device simulator used by customers to build and deploy streaming apps to the platform.
Architected and prototyped Senza Shaka Player and Auto Background, two features that simplified the app integration workflow and were shipped as part of the SDK.
Built an automated engagement analytics pipeline using Google Analytics and Claude, giving the sales team per-company behavioral data and warm lead identification.
Managed the implementation of Senza Direct, a self-service SaaS onboarding flow that reduced time-to-first-app for new customers.
Authored comprehensive developer documentation, developed 40+ sample code apps, and recorded 20+ video tutorials.
Organized an external hackathon to validate the developer experience with real users.
Developed AI-powered tools that let developers query platform documentation and manage their device fleet conversationally.
Helped enterprise sales team close deals by demonstrating AI-assisted development workflows.

intro: Member of Cisco's advanced technology team focused on future directions for connected TV and multi-device experiences. Built proof-of-concept streaming and second-screen platforms used to engage potential customers across cable, satellite, and emerging device ecosystems.
bullets: Led development of a cross-platform TV developer ecosystem, enabling app developers to target cable and satellite platforms from a single unified API.
Built second-screen companion apps for social TV experiences, including a Facebook-powered TV guide and a synchronized companion app for Top Gear.
Conceived and built Flare, an indoor location platform for wearables, with SDKs for six platforms, developer documentation on Cisco DevNet, and a live demo at NRF.
Presented platforms at trade shows, developer conferences, and hackathons.
